heading: Transparency
label: 1153a Transparency
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the EB–5 Reform and Integrity Act of 2022, and also as part of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2022, and not as part of the Immigration and Nationality Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Employees of the Department of Homeland Security, including the Secretary of Homeland Security, the Secretary’s counselors, the Assistant Secretary for the Private Sector, the Director of U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services, counselors to such Director, and the Chief of the Immigrant Investor Programs Office (or any successor to such Office) at U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services, shall act impartially and may not give preferential treatment to any entity, organization, or individual in connection with any aspect of the immigrant visa program described in .section 1153(b)(5) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a regional center;

children.label: B
children.text: a new commercial enterprise;

children.label: C
children.text: a job-creating entity; or

children.label: D
children.text: any person or entity associated with such regional center, new commercial enterprise, or job-creating entity; and

meeting or communicating with persons associated with the entities listed in paragraph (1), at the request of such persons, in a manner not available to or accorded to all other petitioners, applicants, and seekers of benefits under such immigrant visa program.

children.heading: Written communication
children.label: 1 Written communication
children.text: Employees of the Department of Homeland Security, including the officials listed in subsection (a), shall include, in the record of proceeding for a case under , actual or electronic copies of all case-specific written communication, including emails from government and private accounts, with non-Department persons or entities advocating for regional center applications or individual petitions under such section that are pending on or after (other than routine communications with other agencies of the Federal Government regarding the case, including communications involving background checks and litigation defense).section 1153(b)(5) of this titleMarch 15, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: the conversation shall be recorded; or

children.label: B
children.text: detailed minutes of the session shall be taken and included in the record of proceeding.

children.label: i
children.text: the affected party has been given notice of such evidence; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if such evidence is derogatory, the affected party has been given an opportunity to respond to the evidence.

children.heading: Information from law enforcement, intelligence agencies, or confidential sources
children.label: B Information from law enforcement, intelligence agencies, or confidential sources

children.heading: Law enforcement or intelligence agencies
children.label: i Law enforcement or intelligence agencies
children.text: Evidence received from law enforcement or intelligence agencies may not be made part of the record of proceeding without the consent of the relevant agency or law enforcement entity.

children.heading: Whistleblowers, confidential sources, or intelligence agencies
children.label: ii Whistleblowers, confidential sources, or intelligence agencies
children.text: Evidence received from whistleblowers, other confidential sources, or the intelligence community that is included in the record of proceeding and considered in adjudicative proceedings shall be handled in a manner that does not reveal the identity of the whistleblower or confidential source, or reveal classified information.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No case-specific communication with persons or entities that are not part of the Department of Homeland Security may be considered in the adjudication of an application or petition under unless the communication is included in the record of proceeding of the case.section 1153(b)(5) of this title

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 2 Waiver
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security may waive the requirement under paragraph (1) only in the interests of national security or for investigative or law enforcement purposes.

children.label: A
children.text: with inquiries regarding specific petitions or applications under the immigrant visa program described in ; orsection 1153(b)(5) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: seeking information that is not case-specific about the immigrant visa program described in such section 1153(b)(5).

children.heading: Communication only through appropriate channels or offices
children.label: 2 Communication only through appropriate channels or offices

children.heading: Announcement of appropriate channels of communication
children.label: A Announcement of appropriate channels of communication
i
children.text: the email address or equivalent channel described in paragraph (1);

children.label: ii
children.text: the National Customer Service Center, or any successor to such Center; or

children.label: iii
children.text: the Office of Public Engagement, Immigrant Investor Program Office, including the Stakeholder Engagement Branch, or any successors to those Offices or that Branch.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Employees of the Department of Homeland Security shall direct communications described in subparagraph (A) to the channels of communication or offices listed in clauses (i) through (iii) of subparagraph (A).

children.label: I
children.text: any person from communicating with the Ombudsman of U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services regarding the immigrant investor program under ; orsection 1153(b)(5) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the Ombudsman from resolving problems regarding such immigrant investor program pursuant to the authority granted under .section 272 of title 6

children.label: I
children.text: all communications described in subparagraph (A) and communications from Members of Congress, which shall reference the date, time, and subject of the communication, and the identity of the Department official, if any, to whom the inquiry was forwarded;

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to written communications described in subsection (c)(1), the date on which the communication was received, the identities of the sender and addressee, and the subject of the communication; and

children.label: III
children.text: with respect to oral communications described in subsection (c)(2), the date on which the communication occurred, the participants in the conversation or meeting, and the subject of the communication.

children.heading: Transparency
children.label: ii Transparency
children.text: The log of communications described in clause (i) shall be made publicly available in accordance with (commonly known as the “Freedom of Information Act”).section 552 of title 5

children.heading: Publication of information
children.label: 3 Publication of information
children.text: Not later than 30 days after a person or entity inquiring about a specific case or generally about the immigrant visa program described in receives, as a result of a communication with an official of the Department of Homeland Security, generally applicable information that is not case-specific about program requirements or administration that has not been made publicly available by the Department, the Director of U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services shall publish such information on the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services website as an update to the relevant Frequently Asked Questions page or by some other comparable mechanism.section 1153(b)(5) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any person who intentionally violates the prohibition on preferential treatment under this section or intentionally violates the reporting requirements under subsection (c) shall be disciplined in accordance with paragraph (2).

children.heading: Sanctions
children.label: 2 Sanctions
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Secretary of Homeland Security shall establish a graduated set of sanctions based on the severity of the violation referred to in paragraph (1), which may include, in addition to any criminal or civil penalties that may be imposed, written reprimand, suspension, demotion, or removal.March 15, 2022

children.heading: Rule of construction regarding classified information
children.label: g Rule of construction regarding classified information
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to modify any law, regulation, or policy regarding the handling or disclosure of classified information.

children.heading: Rule of construction regarding private right of action
children.label: h Rule of construction regarding private right of action
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to create or authorize a private right of action to challenge a decision of an employee of the Department of Homeland Security.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: i Effective date
children.text: This section, and the amendments made by this section, shall take effect on .March 15, 2022
